name: United States
cDesc: Global reserve currency (60% of world trade)

sdgGaps.text: 81% fossil dependency. NDC requires 50%+ renewable electricity by 2030. Current trajectory misses by 15 percentage points.
#1 cumulative emitter. Paris pledge requires cutting 1.8 GtCO₂/yr by 2030. Current pace insufficient.
Climate finance commitments chronically underfunded — $11.4B pledged vs $100B global target.

sdgActions.label: Immediate
sdgActions.text: Reinstate EPA methane regulations. Accelerate IRA clean energy tax credit deployment to utilities.

sdgActions.label: Medium term
sdgActions.text: Fund $50B Green Climate Fund contribution. Pass carbon pricing legislation by 2027.

sdgActions.label: Structural
sdgActions.text: Ratify loss and damage framework. As #1 cumulative emitter, highest obligation to fund climate adaptation in vulnerable nations.

name: China
pledge: Paris NDC: Peak emissions before 2030, carbon neutral by 2060

sdgGaps.text: 56% coal in energy mix. Pledge to peak before 2030 requires coal to stop growing by 2025 — still expanding.
Coal causes 1.4M premature deaths annually — SDG 3 failure embedded in energy policy.
Carbon neutrality by 2060 requires 4–5% annual reductions from 2030. No credible sectoral roadmap published.

sdgActions.label: Immediate
sdgActions.text: Impose hard cap on new coal plant approvals. Enforce retirement of coal plants over 30 years old.

sdgActions.label: Medium term
sdgActions.text: Publish detailed 2060 neutrality sectoral roadmap. Extend ETS carbon market to heavy industry by 2026.

sdgActions.label: Structural
sdgActions.text: Redirect Belt and Road Initiative to export clean energy rather than coal infrastructure — currently 40% of BRI energy projects are fossil.

name: European Union
pledge: European Green Deal: 55% below 1990 levels by 2030, net zero 2050

sdgGaps.text: 68% fossil dependency. Green Deal targets 45% renewable electricity by 2030. Pace achievable but requires sustained investment.
Carbon transition costs disproportionately burden lower-income households — energy poverty affects 41M Europeans.
CBAM creates trade friction with developing nations who cannot afford clean energy transitions.

sdgActions.label: Immediate
sdgActions.text: Expand CBAM scope to all industrial goods by 2026. Use revenues to fund Just Transition Fund for affected developing nations.

sdgActions.label: Medium term
sdgActions.text: Accelerate REPowerEU — 45% renewable target by 2030 requires doubling current installation rate.

sdgActions.label: Structural
sdgActions.text: Reform EU energy poverty directive. Pair carbon pricing with household dividends to prevent green transition deepening inequality.

name: India
pledge: Paris NDC: 45% emissions intensity reduction by 2030, 50% renewable power

sdgGaps.text: 77% fossil dependency. 50% renewable target by 2030 requires adding 450 GW — financing gap of $150B remains unfilled.
600M rural Indians depend on agriculture. Climate disruption to monsoon directly undermines SDG 1.
45% intensity reduction achievable but requires green financing at 4–5x current rates. Debt trap risk is real.

sdgActions.label: Immediate
sdgActions.text: Negotiate concessional climate finance at under 1% interest from Green Climate Fund to replace market-rate borrowing.

sdgActions.label: Medium term
sdgActions.text: Accelerate domestic solar manufacturing to reduce Chinese panel dependency. Engineering capacity exists — needs policy incentives.

sdgActions.label: Structural
sdgActions.text: Demand loss and damage compensation from historical emitters. India's 2.8t CO₂/capita vs USA 17.3t makes the historical inequity legally actionable.

name: Russia
pledge: Paris NDC: 30% below 1990 levels by 2030 (with accounting loopholes)

sdgGaps.text: Paris pledge uses forest sink accounting loopholes. Real reductions near zero. 98% fossil dependency makes SDG 7 completely unmet.
Fossil revenues fund military expansion. Energy weapon use against Europe in 2022 violated SDG 16 principles.
Active obstruction at COP26, 27, 28 documented. G20 climate finance commitments unfulfilled.

sdgActions.label: Immediate
sdgActions.text: Honor existing Paris pledge without accounting loopholes. Publish transparent national emissions inventory with independent verification.

sdgActions.label: Medium term
sdgActions.text: Develop Siberian wind and solar potential — world-class renewable resources completely untapped at 2% of energy mix.

sdgActions.label: Structural
sdgActions.text: Re-engage with multilateral climate finance. Arctic development under warming requires international cooperation to remain viable long-term.

name: Brazil
pledge: Paris NDC: Net zero by 2050, halt illegal deforestation by 2030

sdgGaps.text: Amazon deforestation reached 11,568 km² in 2022. Pledge to halt illegal deforestation by 2030 requires sustained enforcement — politically contested.
Net-zero 2050 pledge achievable given 45% renewable base — but only if deforestation stops. Currently deforestation contributes 40% of Brazil's emissions.
Amazon degradation reduces rainfall across South America affecting agricultural productivity for 250M people beyond Brazil's borders.

sdgActions.label: Immediate
sdgActions.text: Fully fund and staff IBAMA environmental enforcement. Deforestation enforcement collapsed 2019–2022 — rebuild institutional capacity now.

sdgActions.label: Medium term
sdgActions.text: Monetise Amazon conservation through Article 6 carbon markets. Brazil could earn $50B+ annually — more than deforestation generates.

sdgActions.label: Structural
sdgActions.text: Diversify hydro with wind and solar to reduce drought vulnerability. Amazon degradation reduces rainfall — hydro dependency is a long-term strategic risk.

name: Saudi Arabia
cDesc: USD-pegged, oil revenues fund 70% of budget
pledge: Paris NDC: 278 Mt CO₂ reduction by 2030, net zero by 2060

sdgGaps.text: 99% fossil dependency. 22.8t CO₂/capita — highest in simulation. Vision 2030 renewable target of 50% by 2030 is drastically off pace at under 1% today.
Net zero by 2060 pledge has no credible sectoral roadmap. Saudi Aramco expansion plans directly contradict the pledge.
Saudi Arabia actively lobbied to weaken COP fossil fuel phase-out language at COP26, 27, 28. Direct SDG 17 violation.

sdgActions.label: Immediate
sdgActions.text: Stop lobbying to weaken COP fossil fuel phase-out language. Commit to no new oil field development beyond existing approved projects.

sdgActions.label: Medium term
sdgActions.text: Accelerate NEOM and Red Sea Project solar targets. Saudi has world's best solar irradiance — 1% of land in solar panels covers 100% of electricity demand.

sdgActions.label: Structural
sdgActions.text: Use sovereign wealth fund ($600B) to shift to renewable infrastructure investment before reserves become stranded assets.

name: Nigeria
pledge: Paris NDC: 20% unconditional, 47% conditional reduction by 2030

sdgGaps.text: 85M Nigerians lack electricity access — highest energy poverty count in Africa. SDG 7 requires universal access by 2030. Current trajectory misses by 2040+.
Conditional 47% reduction pledge requires $29B in international climate finance that has not materialised. Without it only 20% is feasible.
Vulnerability 0.81 means climate shocks push millions below poverty line annually. Climate adaptation IS poverty reduction for Nigeria.

sdgActions.label: Immediate
sdgActions.text: Invoke loss and damage mechanism at UNFCCC. Nigeria has a legal and moral case — 0.6t CO₂/capita yet faces catastrophic impacts from others' emissions.

sdgActions.label: Medium term
sdgActions.text: Deploy distributed solar mini-grids to reach 85M unelectrified Nigerians. Cheaper than grid extension, no fossil dependency, directly fulfils SDG 7.

sdgActions.label: Structural
sdgActions.text: Reform fossil fuel subsidies ($10B/yr) and redirect to renewable infrastructure. Targeted cash transfers protect poor households while enabling transition.
